english: mduṅ-mkan (mdung mkhan). -- mduṅ rtse-gsúm-pa (mdung rtse gsum pa) trident. -- 2. sting, of insects C., W., mduṅ brgyáb-pa (mdung brgyab pa) to sting. -- 3. gser-mduṅ (gser mdung), dṅul-mduṅ (dngul mdung) prob. the two frontal muscles Med.

a knot, mdúd-pa bór-ba (mdud pa bor ba) frq., dúd-pa (dud pa) Lt., byéd-pa (byed pa) Cs., *gyáb-ċe* (gyab ce) W., to tie or make a knot, sgrol-ba (sgrol ba), ˚grol-ba ('grol ba), to untie (a knot); *ḍól-dud* (dol dud) W. sliding-knot, slip-knot, *śiṅ-dud* (shing dud) W. a regular knot; skra-mdúd (skra mdud) knot or bow of ribbons holding together the long plaits of the women; frq. fig. sér-snai mdúd-pa (ser sna'i mdud pa) bonds of avarice Mil.; *nyiṅ-dud ḍól-ċe* (nying dud dol ce) W. (to untie) to open one's heart to a person; mdúd-pa-ċan (mdud pa can). 1. full of knots, knotty. 2. cloddy (?) S.g. - mdúd-˚dra (mdud 'dra) a disease of the membrum virile, prob. paraphimosis Miṅ.

the fore-part, the front-side of a thing; the vis-à-vis, mdún-gyi nám-mka-la (mdun gyi nam mkha' la) in the heavens before him, over against him, Glr. and elsewh.; mdún-gyis (mdun gyis) adv. coram, face to face, mdún-gyis ltá-ba (mdun gyis lta ba) to behold face to face; gen. c. la, na, du, nas: 1. adv. before it, at it, to it, from it; 2. postp. before, at, to etc.; mdún-la ˚óṅ-ba (mdun la 'ong ba), or sléb-pa (sleb pa) to come up or near, ráṅ-gi mdún-la sleb ma bċug (rang gi mdun la sleb ma bcug) he did not allow (the pursuer) to come near; mdún-du skúr-ba (mdun du skur ba) to send in advance; mdun-du ṗyin-pa (mdun du phyin pa) to come near, to approach; to hasten to Pth.; mi máṅ-po ṭsógs-pai mdún-du (mi mang po tshogs pa'i mdun du) in the presence of a great number of people Dzl. -- sku-mdún-pa (sku mdun pa) a waiting-man, valet de chambre, v. sku (sku). -- mdun-lcóg (mdun lcog) v. lcóg-tse (lcog tse). -- mdun-na-˚don (mdun na 'don) (C. *ḍun-nán-dọn* (*dun nan don*)) 1. Lex. पुरोहित, court-chaplain, domestic chaplain or priest; so prob. also Tar. 58, 17. -- 2. at present: a high civil officer or functionary, = bka-blón (bka' blon), vizier, Stg. and elsewh.
frq. in later lit.; one Lama explained it by mós-pa (mos pa), another by: 1. wife, 2. things, concerns; ˚jig-rtén-gyi mdún-ma ('jig rten gyi mdun ma) = ˚jig-rtén-gyi bya-ba ('jig rten gyi bya ba).
, Sch. also mde-k'a (mde kha), arrow-head B.; mde-súl (mde sul) Cs.: 'the furrows or grooves of an arrow-head'.

1. the lower part of a valley, where it merges into the plain (opp. to ṗu (phu)), = mda (mda'); more frq. the place where one valley opens into another, hence in general: the point where two valleys, roads (lám-mdo (lam mdo)), rivers (čú-mdo (chu mdo)) meet; lám-sraṅ-mdor (lam srang mdor) at the street-corners Dzl.; gsum-mdo (gsum mdo), bźi-mdo (bzhi mdo), cóg-mdo (cog mdo) the point where three, four, several (roads etc.) meet, esp. bźi-mdo (bzhi mdo) a crossing, cross-road, as a place of incantations; mdo (mdo) prop. n. (in full: dar-rtse-mdo (dar rtse mdo)) province of the eastern part of Tibet, v. kams (khams); *do-ru* (*do ru*) in C. used as postp. = near, with, by, *ne ˚do-ru* (*ne 'do ru*) with me, *yul-gyi ˚do-ru* (*yul gyi 'do ru*) near the village. -- 2. Ssk. सूत्र, aphorism, short sentence or rule, axiom; hence mdó-ru (mdo ru), mdor (mdor), mdó-tsam sdu-ba (mdo tsam sdu ba) to contract, abridge, epitomize, to give only the main points, frq.; mdor(-sdu)-na (mdor sdu na) in short, in general, altogether, on an average, denique, frq. -- 3. Sūtra, in the more recent Buddhist sense, religious treatise or dissertation, a sacred writing, mdo-sdé (mdo sde) a collection of Sūtras, a part of the Kangyur; mdo-sdé-pa (mdo sde pa), mdo-sde-˚dzin (mdo sde 'dzin) Sautrāntika, a school of philosophers, v. Tar.; mdo-máṅ (mdo mang) title of several collections of Sūtras; in quoting passages: mdó-la (mdo la), mdó-las (mdo las), in the mdo, according to the mdo (viz. is said, is written etc.) Stg.; mdo-sṅob (mdo sngob) giving a benediction to the host for his entertainment Mil., cf. Köpp. I, 143. At present a distinction is to be made between mdoi (mdo'i) or dbú-mai lam (dbu ma'i lam), and sṅágs-kyi lam (sngags kyi lam), i. e. between the doctrine of the sacred writings and a faithful and systematic study of them, -- and of the more modern mysticism, which is mixed up with Siwaism, and seeks to obtain spiritual gifts by means of witchcraft, thus saving trouble and time; v. Was. (142. 177), Köpp. II, 29. -- 4. Cs. mdó-ċan (mdo can) prudent, mdo-med (mdo med) imprudent, cf. ˚do ('do).
the tibetanized डोली Hind. sedan-chair Pth.
